title: The Pitch

jokes.text: 8:15. Roland still isn't here yet. You know if we're a half hour late for this pitch meeting we'll be laughed out of the room.
jokes.setup: Johnny worries about being late and unprofessional
jokes.punchline: Given their past failures, being 'laughed out of the room' is probably accurate
jokes.explanation: Classic Johnny anxiety with underlying irony about their actual situation

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm going to be late. This is exactly the kind of thing that makes me look unprofessional.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, given our track record, being laughed out of the room is probably accurate.

jokes.text: Mr. Rose, you're stressing me out. And I just chugged a bottle of CBD oil.
jokes.setup: Stevie mentions being stressed
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's already consumed an entire bottle of CBD oil
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie delivery - deadpan revelation of extreme pre-meeting anxiety self-medication

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm stressed.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I've already consumed an entire bottle of CBD oil.

jokes.text: Help? - I said support, dear.
jokes.setup: David mishears or deliberately misinterprets Moira
jokes.punchline: Moira's careful distinction between 'help' and 'support'
jokes.explanation: Typical Moira precision with words, David's sarcastic interpretation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I need your help with something.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I can't help you. But I can offer you my support.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Those are the same thing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, help is doing something. Support is just being here while you do it.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: So you're refusing to help me, but you're willing to stand nearby?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Exactly. I'm very supportive that way.

jokes.text: There is a jet? Can I come? - I already asked. - I'll swap out with someone.
jokes.setup: Alexis discovers there's a private jet
jokes.punchline: David already tried to get on it and is now offering to trade places
jokes.explanation: Shows both siblings' opportunistic nature and David's calculated approach

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Wait, there's a private jet?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, I already tried to get on it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I will trade you places if you want.

jokes.text: Johnny, woo! Look at me. I'm you. I can't believe your suit fits me, we're completely different sizes. And I have a much more athletic build.
jokes.setup: Roland wearing Johnny's suit and commenting on fit
jokes.punchline: Claims he has a 'much more athletic build' while clearly being less fit
jokes.explanation: Pure Roland delusion about his physical condition compared to Johnny

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: This suit fits me perfectly. I have a much more athletic build than Johnny.

jokes.text: He looks the same age as the day I met him. - Oh, I'm sure he's just as handsome as he's ever been.
jokes.setup: Moira making a comment about Roland's appearance
jokes.punchline: Alexis's diplomatically vague response that doesn't actually compliment him
jokes.explanation: Polite deflection disguised as a compliment - classic diplomatic non-answer

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Roland, you're looking... well.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You know, that's so nice of you to say that.

jokes.text: Rollie, there's mints in your pocket, there was a lot of onions in the omelette.
jokes.setup: Moira addressing Roland's breath before the meeting
jokes.punchline: Bluntly tells him about his onion breath in front of everyone
jokes.explanation: Moira's brutal honesty disguised as helpfulness

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Roland, before we go in there, we need to talk about your breath. It's absolutely devastating. Did you have onions for lunch?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: What? No, I didn't.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, something died in your mouth, and I'm not going in that meeting until you've dealt with it.

jokes.text: Goodbye, my conquistadors! We are all relying on you... No! We're cheering you on!
jokes.setup: Moira giving a dramatic sendoff
jokes.punchline: Catches herself putting too much pressure on them and awkwardly corrects
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira moment - dramatic flourish followed by immediate damage control

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm going to miss you all so very much. You've become like family to me, and I expect you to make me proud, to reach for the stars, to achieve greatness, to—

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: —or, you know, just be happy. That's fine too. Whatever works for you.

jokes.text: Can we maybe stop with the drum circle? Just-I'm just not feeling great. Are private planes always that bumpy?
jokes.setup: Stevie addressing noise and her discomfort
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's motion sick on what should be a luxury experience
jokes.explanation: Stevie's typical understated complaint about an ironic situation

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I can't do this anymore. The constant swaying, the noise, the smell of diesel fuel mixed with someone's cologne that costs more than my car.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm motion sick on a yacht. A YACHT, David. This is supposed to be a luxury experience and I'm green like I'm on a fishing boat in a storm.

jokes.text: Agree? - Agree. - Sure.
jokes.setup: Johnny looking for enthusiastic agreement
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly lackluster responses from his team
jokes.explanation: Shows the team's lack of enthusiasm despite Johnny's confidence

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: This is going to be our best quarter yet, isn't it?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Sure, yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I guess so.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland? Stevie? A little more enthusiasm, please?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yep.

jokes.text: Uh, do you know if there is water where we're going? 'Cause I just got a sudden case of dry mouth.
jokes.setup: Stevie nervously asking about water
jokes.punchline: Admits her anxiety is causing physical symptoms
jokes.explanation: Stevie's anxiety manifesting in awkward small talk with the assistant

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm getting a little anxious about the water situation. Like, my stomach's in knots, my palms are sweating, and I think I'm having heart palpitations.

jokes.text: What? - Yeah, unfortunately, he had to board a flight last night to Johannesburg, but he sends his best. - Um, we can always wait 'til he gets back. - He's there for a week.
jokes.setup: Learning the main contact isn't there
jokes.punchline: Johnny's desperate offer to wait is shot down - he's gone for a week
jokes.explanation: Classic setup disappointment followed by Johnny's pathetic attempt to salvage

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: When will he be back?

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.line: He's gone for a week.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I can wait.

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.line: Not here, you can't.

jokes.text: my parents used to take me to Rose Video all the time when I was a kid. - Oh. - I always loved the free popcorn. Nice touch.
jokes.setup: Ruth mentions fond memories of Rose Video
jokes.punchline: The 'nice touch' she remembers is free popcorn - a minor business expense
jokes.explanation: Bittersweet reminder of the Rose family's fall from their video store empire

dialogue.speaker: Ruth
dialogue.line: You know, I have such fond memories of Rose Apothecary. There was this nice touch you had.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Really? What was that?

dialogue.speaker: Ruth
dialogue.line: Free popcorn.

jokes.text: I like your offices. Office.
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to compliment the space
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects herself - it's just one office, not multiple
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie awkwardness - trying to be polite but immediately undermining herself

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: This is a really nice... I mean, it's just an office.

jokes.text: You see a few cherished items being neatly folded into a piece of luggage and you immediately leap to conclusions.
jokes.setup: Moira defending her actions
jokes.punchline: Describes packing in the most elaborate way possible while denying it
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - overwrought description that actually confirms what she's denying

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I am not packing. I am simply organizing my wardrobe into a series of carefully curated, travel-ready ensembles. Each outfit has been thoughtfully composed to maximize versatility while maintaining my signature aesthetic. I've color-coordinated by season, fabric weight, and occasion. The steamer trunks are merely a storage solution—a temporary one, I might add—and the fact that they're currently stationed by the door is purely coincidental. So no, I am absolutely not packing.

jokes.text: God forbid I find myself once again being forcefully evacuated and given no time to do a thorough sweep.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her packing as precaution
jokes.punchline: References their previous sudden departure with dramatic language
jokes.explanation: Moira's PTSD from their financial collapse expressed in her typical grandiose terms

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I learned my lesson the last time. One moment you're living in a penthouse, the next you're living in a barn. So I keep a bag packed at all times, just in case lightning strikes twice.

jokes.text: Alexis, let's not count our poultry before it's incubated
jokes.setup: Moira trying to say a common phrase
jokes.punchline: Mangles 'don't count your chickens before they hatch' into something more elaborate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira malapropism - makes a simple saying needlessly complex and wrong

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: One mustn't prematurely enumerate one's poultry prior to the emergence of said fowl from their calcium-based encasements.

jokes.text: I forgot my wallet and keys and phone. Wait. Why-why are you doing that? - Um, apparently when dad signs the deal, we are moving to New York. - What? Well, who's moving to New York? - Mom and Dad. And me. - And nobody thought to include me in this plan?
jokes.setup: David discovers the family moving plans
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's been completely left out of the discussion
jokes.explanation: David's mounting horror as he realizes his exclusion from family plans

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're all moving? And nobody thought to include me in this conversation?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: David, we were going to tell you.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: When? After you'd already left? I'm literally standing right here.

jokes.text: Okay. You would have no way of knowing this, but usually when someone sends a jet for you, it means he's very interested. Trust me. And Tiger Woods.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining her expertise in private jets
jokes.punchline: Casually name-drops Tiger Woods as if she has comparable experience
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis moment - casual celebrity reference as if she's on their level

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, I've been on so many private jets that I basically know everything about them. I've flown with Tiger Woods, I've flown with... well, mostly Tiger Woods.

jokes.text: Oh, David, you try finding an affordable one-bedroom in a place that's not Atlantic City.
jokes.setup: David questioning the apartment sharing plan
jokes.punchline: Alexis's weirdly specific knowledge of cheap housing markets
jokes.explanation: Unexpected specificity about Atlantic City reveals Alexis's odd practical knowledge

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're really going to move in with Patrick?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, I've lived in twelve different countries, David. I know all the cheap housing markets. Budapest's great right now, but honestly, the best deal is definitely a studio apartment above a depanneur in Montreal.

jokes.text: Oh my God, your bedroom would have one of those little beds that comes down from the wall.
jokes.setup: Alexis describing apartment features enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: Describes a Murphy bed as if it's a luxury feature
jokes.explanation: Alexis's tone-deaf enthusiasm for what's actually a space-saving necessity

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: And look at this Murphy bed! It's like having two rooms in one. During the day it's a spacious living area, and at night it transforms into a bedroom. It's basically architecture for people who can't commit to anything.

jokes.text: Well, good afternoon, everyone. I'd like to say, long time no see, but Mike's not here and we've just met.
jokes.setup: Johnny opening the pitch meeting with a greeting
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledges the awkwardness of the situation
jokes.explanation: Johnny's awkward attempt to address the elephant in the room

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, hello. I know this is awkward.

jokes.text: Hope the jet was Johnny Rose approved. - Oh. - Although I guess at this point, coach would be Johnny Rose approved? - Quite the tumble you took there, Johnny.
jokes.setup: Investors making small talk about the flight
jokes.punchline: Brutally reference Johnny's financial fall with mock sympathy
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor from the investors mocking Johnny's reduced circumstances

dialogue.speaker: Bryce
dialogue.line: So, how was your flight?

dialogue.speaker: Tripp
dialogue.line: Oh, you know, smooth sailing. First class all the way.

dialogue.speaker: Bryce
dialogue.line: Must be nice. I heard Johnny Rose had to take the bus here. Well, I assume he did—I'm not sure he can afford first class anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Tripp
dialogue.line: Oh man, that's rough. How far the mighty have fallen, huh?

dialogue.speaker: Bryce
dialogue.line: I know, right? I actually feel bad for the guy. One minute you're a mogul, the next you're...

dialogue.speaker: Tripp
dialogue.line: Completely broke? Living in a small town? Trying to figure out what a Schitt's Creek even is?

jokes.text: Could have happened to any one of us. But thank you for flying us in today.
jokes.setup: Johnny responding to their mocking
jokes.punchline: Graciously deflects their insults while maintaining dignity
jokes.explanation: Johnny's dignified response shows his character growth and grace under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I appreciate the feedback. You know, when you've spent as much time in the hospitality industry as I have, you learn to take criticism with a certain... grace. It's all part of the service.

jokes.text: you might be thinking to yourself these motels are small, how and why should I... We invest? Well, that's a good question. - I should hope so. It's why we're all here.
jokes.setup: Stevie losing her train of thought mid-pitch
jokes.punchline: Investor's sarcastic response to her obvious statement
jokes.explanation: Classic presentation anxiety moment with brutal investor feedback

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: And that's why this business model is going to revolutionize the industry because, you know, people want things and we're going to give them those things.

dialogue.speaker: Bryce
dialogue.line: Wow, so you're saying people want things? That's groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Reinvigoration. [screen freezes] Um, should I just wait until it unfreezes?
jokes.setup: Stevie's presentation technology fails at the key moment
jokes.punchline: Her helpless question as the screen freezes on their main buzzword
jokes.explanation: Murphy's law in action - technology fails at the worst possible moment

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So... does anyone know what 'synergy' means?

jokes.text: Billion dollar company and none of us can stop technology from cacking out, am I right?
jokes.setup: Roland trying to lighten the mood
jokes.punchline: Uses the word 'cacking' in a formal business setting
jokes.explanation: Roland's attempt at relatability using inappropriate informal language

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm just going to say it. This whole thing has me absolutely cacking up!

jokes.text: What are you guys pumpin' in here, pollen? Do you mind if I have some of that water there, please. - Jesus. - Oh gosh! - Roland! What are you doing? - What?
jokes.setup: Roland asking about allergies and requesting water
jokes.punchline: Presumably spills water or makes a mess, shocking everyone
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland clumsiness at the worst possible moment

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Do you have any allergies I should know about?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: No, I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Great, I'll get you some water.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Oh my God!

jokes.text: Oh gals! You shouldn't have. - Jocelyn, are we now moonlighting as town crier? - Oh, no, Moira... - Cheeky gawpus you.
jokes.setup: Moira arriving to what appears to be a celebration
jokes.punchline: Uses 'gawpus' (gawking person) as if it's an endearment
jokes.explanation: Moira's invented endearment that's actually mildly insulting

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What is all this?

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: We're throwing you a surprise party!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, how delightful! You little gawpuses!

jokes.text: Our husbands, at my husband's direction, are currently in New York City, vending their business to the corporate cognizant.
jokes.setup: Moira explaining the business trip
jokes.punchline: Says 'corporate cognizant' instead of 'corporate executives' or similar
jokes.explanation: Moira's unnecessarily complex term for business people

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was in a meeting with some very important corporate cognizant, and I realized I had no idea what they were talking about.

jokes.text: Hence our impending move to the Big Red Delicious.
jokes.setup: Moira referring to New York
jokes.punchline: Calls NYC 'Big Red Delicious' instead of 'Big Apple'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira malapropism - gets the apple concept but makes it unnecessarily specific

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've always called New York the Big Red Delicious. Much more accurate.

jokes.text: Grace! Our timorous grinagog. I think I'll miss you most of all.
jokes.setup: Moira addressing Grace at the farewell
jokes.punchline: Calls her 'timorous grinagog' (fearful grinning person) as an endearment
jokes.explanation: Another invented Moira term that's both elaborate and mildly insulting

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You timorous grinagog.

jokes.text: It looks like three cartoon heads exploding. - Well, then they're blowing people's minds. What's the concern?
jokes.setup: Grace explaining Roland's concerning emoji
jokes.punchline: Moira's overly optimistic interpretation of exploding head emojis
jokes.explanation: Moira's desperate optimism leading to misinterpretation of obvious distress signals

dialogue.speaker: Grace
dialogue.line: Roland sent me an emoji of a head exploding.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, that's wonderful! He must be having such a good time!

dialogue.speaker: Grace
dialogue.line: Moira, that emoji means he's very angry.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I prefer to think he's just very excited about life.

jokes.text: The deal has to happen. Because it has to. Because there is no alternative. Okay? It's as simple as that.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to reassure everyone
jokes.punchline: Her circular logic reveals her desperation rather than confidence
jokes.explanation: Moira's facade cracking to reveal genuine fear about their future

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Everything is going to be fine. How do I know? Because I've decided it will be. And when have my decisions ever been wrong? Well, that's not the point. The point is, I have a very good feeling about this, and my feelings are never wrong. Well, almost never. But when they are wrong, it's usually because someone else made a decision that contradicted my feeling, which means it's not really my feeling that was wrong. You see? Everything will be fine.

jokes.text: I don't know, are you? - No. But you can share my muffin. - Seriously. What took you so long?
jokes.setup: David trying to placate Patrick
jokes.punchline: Patrick's continued irritation despite the muffin peace offering
jokes.explanation: Relationship dynamics - David's weak attempt at appeasement

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I brought you a muffin.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I don't want a muffin.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's a really good muffin.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, a muffin is not going to fix this.

jokes.text: One of them was featured in season 3 of 'Sex and the City'.
jokes.setup: David describing apartment options enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: His selling point is TV show placement rather than actual amenities
jokes.explanation: Perfect David priorities - pop culture cache over practical considerations

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This apartment has been featured in three different television shows.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you know it's good.

jokes.text: I thought you'd be excited. Would half a muffin help? - I'm not sure. - I'm not sure. - Okay. Y-you took it so that's a good sign.
jokes.setup: David trying to cheer up upset Patrick
jokes.punchline: David reading way too much into Patrick accepting food
jokes.explanation: David's desperate optimism and overthinking of small gestures

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what? I think you should eat something. It'll make you feel better.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, did you just... Did you just agree with me? Just like that? No resistance, no counter-argument about how you're 'fine' or how you 'don't need anything'? Patrick, are you okay? Should I be concerned? Are we breaking up?

jokes.text: Well, motels are small hotels without the golf courses in case you... Well, good-good-good. So you're familiar at least.
jokes.setup: Johnny explaining what motels are
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's being condescending and awkwardly backpedals
jokes.explanation: Johnny's nervous over-explanation followed by embarrassed recovery

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: A motel is a place where people stay when they're traveling and they need somewhere to rest their head for the night. It's like a hotel, but more... accessible.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I mean, not that there's anything wrong with that. Some very nice people stay in motels. I'm sure you've stayed in plenty of motels. Do you stay in motels? You probably do.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: What I'm trying to say is, it's a perfectly respectable establishment.

jokes.text: My family and I have been staying in a motel for the past three years. And I wouldn't trade our stay there for anything.
jokes.setup: Johnny using personal experience as sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Claims to love living in a motel out of necessity, not choice
jokes.explanation: Powerful moment - Johnny turning their desperate situation into genuine sales pitch

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I actually love living in a motel. It's convenient, it's clean, and the staff is always there if you need anything.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Of course, I didn't choose this lifestyle — I'm forced to live here because my family lost everything — but that's beside the point.

jokes.text: Johnny, even I was impressed, and I thought you had blown it for all of us. Didn't you have a briefcase?
jokes.setup: Roland praising Johnny's presentation
jokes.punchline: Admits he expected Johnny to fail, then asks about missing briefcase
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland - compliments Johnny while simultaneously insulting him

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: That was a great presentation, Johnny. Really professional. I gotta say, I came in here thinking you were gonna absolutely bomb it.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, thank you, Roland. I appreciate that.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, but you didn't. You were great. Say, uh, you didn't happen to see a briefcase around here, did you? Brown leather?

jokes.text: What was in that briefcase anyway? - Nothing. You told me to look intimidating. So I brought a briefcase.
jokes.setup: Stevie asking about Roland's forgotten briefcase
jokes.punchline: Roland reveals it was completely empty, just for show
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland logic - literal interpretation of 'look intimidating'

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: What was even in that briefcase?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Nothing. Absolutely nothing. I just carried it around to look important.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: So you've been walking around with an empty briefcase this whole time?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: That's right. It's all about the perception, Stevie. People see a briefcase, they think you've got it all figured out.

jokes.text: First a video store, now a motel chain. What? He wants to reinvent the pager next?
jokes.setup: Investor dismissing Johnny's business ideas
jokes.punchline: Mocks him for focusing on 'dead' industries
jokes.explanation: Cruel but accurate observation about Johnny's business pattern

dialogue.speaker: Bryce
dialogue.line: You're trying to build a business in industries that are essentially dead. It's like trying to start a buggy whip company in 2019.

jokes.text: Do you people know what it took for Johnny Rose to come back and do this? After being away for all that time? Shame on you. Shame on all of you.
jokes.setup: Stevie defending Johnny after overhearing insults
jokes.punchline: Launches into passionate defense despite being naturally deadpan
jokes.explanation: Massive character moment - reserved Stevie becoming fiercely protective

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You know what? Johnny Rose is one of the most thoughtful, genuine people I know. He cares deeply about the people around him, he's funny, he's smart, and he's done nothing but try to help everyone in this town. So maybe you could show him some respect.

jokes.text: Okay. I just realized you guys are flying us home. So I apologize for my outburst, but I don't regret it.
jokes.setup: Stevie's passionate defense of Johnny
jokes.punchline: Suddenly remembers practical consequences but stands by her words
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedy beat - righteous anger meeting practical reality, but maintaining dignity

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Johnny is a good man. He's kind, he's thoughtful, he's one of the most genuine people I know.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: And anyone who doesn't see that is—

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Wait, sorry. Is Johnny in the room? Because I don't want him to think I'm getting soft on him.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: But yes. Everything I said. He's great.

jokes.text: The man's a legend.
jokes.setup: Stevie concluding her defense of Johnny
jokes.punchline: Simple, declarative statement delivered with Stevie's typical understated delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie moment - maximum impact with minimum words

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: He's not an idiot, he's just Canadian.

jokes.text: To Whom It May Concern: There's a man out there, my husband, he's out there trying to build back what we've lost. And... I'm sorry; 5'10', slow talker...
jokes.setup: Moira beginning some kind of spiritual appeal
jokes.punchline: Describes Johnny as if she's filing a missing person report with the universe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moira absurdism - treating prayer like customer service complaint

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Dear Universe, I'm writing to report a missing person. Male, approximately six feet tall, perpetually unimpressed expression, answers to Johnny Rose. Last seen wearing something from the clearance rack at a department store. If spotted, please return immediately. He's been missing his sense of humor for approximately thirty years.

jokes.text: John, we're here for you. Oh, Alexis your hands as dry as an autumn leaf. - Eww.
jokes.setup: Moira in the middle of emotional prayer
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by Alexis's dry hands
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira moment - serious emotional gesture derailed by superficial concern

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Dear God, I come before you in this moment of profound vulnerability, seeking your divine guidance and—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, can I use your hand cream? Mine are like, so dry right now.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, I am in the middle of a spiritual communion.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, but seriously, look at this. It's like the Sahara.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Have you considered a hydrating mask? There's a wonderful Icelandic one that—actually, those cuticles are concerning. Yes, use the hand cream.

jokes.text: I really shouldn't be saying this on company soil, but two of the junior partners and I are in the process of starting our own firm.
jokes.setup: Ruth offering alternative after the rejection
jokes.punchline: Admits to corporate betrayal while literally standing in her company's office
jokes.explanation: Corporate intrigue with awareness of the ironic setting

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, if you won't take the position, perhaps you'd consider a role in our new division. We're expanding into... let's call it 'lifestyle curation.'

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Of course, I should mention that I've been embezzling from the company for three years, and I'm doing it right now, in this office, while wearing Valentino.

jokes.text: It's like 'Deal or No Deal', but with the rest of our lives.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn commenting on waiting for the business results
jokes.punchline: Compares their life-changing moment to a game show
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly insightful comparison from usually naive Jocelyn

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: It's like we're waiting for the results of a game show, but instead of winning a car, we're winning our lives back.

jokes.text: So you're like a businesswoman now. - Damn right I am. - I sealed the deal. It was really me.
jokes.setup: David acknowledging Stevie's success
jokes.punchline: Roland immediately trying to take credit
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moments - Stevie's confidence, Roland's delusion

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Stevie, you've been doing an amazing job at the motel.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Thank you, David.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, she had a good teacher. I showed her everything she knows about running that place.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Roland, you don't even work there.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Not officially.

jokes.text: We only have Zampagne. - Zampagne for all!
jokes.setup: David noting they only have cheap champagne substitute
jokes.punchline: Moira enthusiastically embracing the inferior product
jokes.explanation: Family's reduced circumstances celebrated with appropriate enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We only have this cheap champagne substitute.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, David, don't be such a snob. This is perfectly adequate. In fact, I find it rather delightful. It's like they took all the pretension out of champagne and left only the joy.

tmdb_overview: Johnny, Stevie and Roland head to New York for their pitch meeting, while the family anxiously awaits their return.
insight: Final season pivot trades laughs for heart; cringe comedy carries a lower-density sendoff.
